content:
Match List I with List II.

List I (Atoms) List II (Properties)
A. He I. High electron affinity
B. Cl II. Most electropositive element
C. Ca III. Strong reducing agent
D. Li IV. Highest ionisation energy

Choose the correct answer from the options given below.
explanation:
The correct match is A-IV, B-I, C-II, D-III

$\bullet$ He has highest ionisation energy due to inert gas configuration.

$\bullet$ Cl has highest electron affinity among given atoms.

$\bullet$ Ca is the most electropositive element among given atoms.

$\bullet$ Li is strongest reducing agent among given atoms.

content: Which of the following order in respect to ionisation energy is correct?
explanation:
Ionisation energy is the energy required to remove an electron from outer shell of an atom. Since, on moving from left to right in a period, size decreases, thus larger amount of energy is required to remove an electron, i.e. ionisation energy increases.

Although the size of O -atom is smaller as compared to N but the ionisation energy of N is higher.

This is because in case of $N$, the electron is to be removed from stable half filled $2 p^3$ orbital. Hence, the correct order is

$$\mathrm{N}>\mathrm{O}<\mathrm{F}$$

content: Which of the following is false regarding shielding effect?

options.content: The magnitude of screening effects depends upon the number of outer electrons.
The screening effect and effective nuclear charge are very closely related.
Screening effect of all the inner electrons is not equal.
The screening effect of $$d$$ and $$f$$ orbitals is taken as almost constant.

explanation: The magnitude of screening effects depend upon the number of inner electrons. Higher the number of inner electrons, greater shall be the value of screening effect.

content: Which is largest in size in aqueous solution?
explanation:
Size of ions: $$\mathrm{Li}^{+}<\mathrm{Na}^{+}<\mathrm{Rb}^{+}<\mathrm{Cs}^{+}$$

Size of hydrated ion $$\mathrm{Li}^{+}>\mathrm{Na}^{+}>\mathrm{Rb}^{+}>\mathrm{Cs}^{+}$$

Smaller the size of ion higher will be the hydration and hence larger will be the size of the hydrated ion (in aqueous solution).

content: Arrange the following in order of their electronegativity.
explanation:
Across the period, electronegativity increases and down the group, it decreases. Hence, the correct order of electronegativity is

$$\mathrm{F}>\mathrm{O}>\mathrm{N}>\mathrm{C}$$

content: How does electron affinity change when we move from left to right in a period in the Periodic Table?

options.content: It first increases and then decreases

explanation: Electron affinity increases when we move from left to right in a period.

content: Which of the following statements is not correct?

options.content: lonisation energy increases on going down a group in the Periodic Table
Among alkaline earth metals, reducing character increases down the group
Fluorine is the most electronegative element
Metallic character increases on going down a group in the Periodic Table

explanation: Correct statement is ionisation energy decreases on going down a group in the Periodic Table.
